skill.name: plot-consistency-checker
skill.description: Analyzes narrative plot points, timelines, and story events to identify inconsistencies, plot holes, continuity errors, and pacing issues. Use when the user needs help ensuring their story remains logically consistent and well-paced.
skill.summary: Checks story timelines, character logic, plot threads, world rules, point of view, and pacing.
skill.author: AbdulSamad94

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but manual review found only Markdown fences, table examples, and narrative editing prose in SKILL.md. No executable script, network access, shell invocation, secret access, prompt injection, or data exfiltration behavior was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The scanner interpreted Markdown backtick fences and report examples as Ruby or shell execution. These locations are static documentation examples and do not execute commands or interpolate user input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are Markdown code fence delimiters or adjacent static examples. There is no Ruby, shell command, command substitution, or executable script in these locations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Narrative Prose Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged words inside the skill description, analysis checklist, and fictional examples as weak cryptography indicators. The reviewed content discusses plot, character evidence, and Markdown formatting, not cryptographic algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows story-analysis text such as descriptions, evidence sections, and character examples. No MD5, SHA1, cipher selection, password hashing, or security-sensitive cryptographic code appears in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Story Examples Flagged as System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged lines that mention user requests, story evidence, recommendations, and fictional dialogue. These are examples for manuscript analysis and do not query host, file, network, or environment information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is plain manuscript-review guidance and quoted fictional text. There are no commands such as uname, whoami, env, ipconfig, or filesystem enumeration.

content.value_statement: Long stories often develop timeline gaps, unresolved threads, and point-of-view errors. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through a structured continuity review.
actual_capabilities: Creates trackers for timelines, character actions, plot threads, and world rules.
Identifies timeline conflicts, impossible travel, age errors, and day or season mismatches.
Checks character motivation, knowledge, physical description, and location continuity.
Finds unresolved plot threads, missing cause and effect, and overly convenient coincidences.
Reviews point-of-view consistency and pacing across chapters or scenes.
Produces prioritized findings with evidence, impact, and suggested fixes.
limitations: Requires the user to provide story text, synopsis, timeline details, or world rules.
Cannot verify continuity outside the material supplied by the user.
May need author judgment for intentional mysteries, unreliable narrators, or time loops.
Does not replace a human developmental editor or sensitivity reader.

use_cases.title: Audit a Draft Novel
use_cases.description: Review multiple chapters for timeline breaks, unresolved setups, and character continuity problems before a major revision.

use_cases.title: Check Point of View
use_cases.description: Find accidental head-hopping, impossible narrator knowledge, and scenes that break first-person or limited-third rules.

use_cases.title: Prepare Beta Reader Notes
use_cases.description: Turn broad reader feedback into a focused list of plot, pacing, and world-building issues to investigate.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Continuity Check
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a short excerpt
prompt_templates.prompt: Check this scene for plot consistency. Focus on timeline, character actions, and any details that contradict earlier information I provide.

prompt_templates.title: Chapter Timeline Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze these chapters for timeline problems. Build a simple event order, flag impossible durations, and suggest fixes for each issue.

prompt_templates.title: Character and POV Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this draft for character consistency and point-of-view violations. Track what each character knows, where they are, and whether the narration reveals impossible knowledge.

prompt_templates.title: Full Plot Consistency Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a full plot consistency report for this manuscript excerpt. Include critical, moderate, and minor issues, evidence, reader impact, pacing notes, and priority fixes.

output_examples.input: Five fantasy chapters with a journey from one city to another.
output_examples.output: The report flags an impossible overnight journey, explains the earlier distance reference, and suggests adding a time skip or transport explanation.

output_examples.input: A first-person romance scene with information from another character's private conversation.
output_examples.output: The review identifies a point-of-view violation and suggests revealing the information through dialogue, discovery, or later reflection.

output_examples.input: A mystery outline with several clues and suspect scenes.
output_examples.output: The analysis lists unresolved clues, tracks suspect knowledge, and separates intentional red herrings from unsupported reveals.

best_practices: Provide chapter numbers, scene order, character lists, and any known world rules.
Mark intentional contradictions so the review treats them as design choices.
Fix critical logic breaks before polishing minor description errors.
anti_patterns: Submitting isolated scenes without the earlier details they depend on.
Treating every flagged ambiguity as an error without considering author intent.
Asking for pacing advice without sharing chapter length, scene purpose, or conflict level.

faq.question: Can this review a full manuscript?
faq.answer: Yes, if the text fits the active tool context. For long books, review chapters or arcs in batches.

faq.question: Does it only find plot holes?
faq.answer: No. It also checks timelines, character behavior, world rules, point of view, and pacing.

faq.question: Can it handle fantasy or science fiction rules?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide the rules, limits, exceptions, and important prior examples from the story.

faq.question: Will it rewrite my scenes?
faq.answer: The skill focuses on diagnosis and fix options. You can ask for revised text after reviewing the findings.

faq.question: Can it recognize intentional contradictions?
faq.answer: Yes, when you label them as intentional. Otherwise, it may flag them for author review.

faq.question: Is it useful before beta readers?
faq.answer: Yes. It can remove obvious continuity errors before readers focus on character, emotion, and structure.
